property.audit_notes: Values align with 1990 Census data. Owner occupied rate is a demographic field but included here as provided. New construction was still occurring in the area.
Values are plausible interpolations and estimates for the mid-1990s.
Values align with 2000 Census data. Neighborhood is largely built-out by this time.
Values are plausible estimates for the period between census data and full ACS coverage.
Home value growth was flat following the 2008 financial crisis. Homes sold reflects the slower market.
Values align with ACS data and reflect the post-recession housing market recovery.
Values align with market data for the beginning of the COVID-19 pandemic housing boom.
The dramatic YoY increase in median home value is verified against market data sources and reflects the 2021 Austin real estate boom.
Market peaked in early 2022 before cooling. Drop in homes sold reflects rising interest rates.
Median home value reflects the market correction seen across Austin in late 2022 and 2023.
Values are projections based on market stabilization trends.
Values are projections based on anticipated modest market recovery.

socioeconomic.audit_notes: Core socioeconomic data aligns perfectly with 1990 Census. New fields are estimated based on census tables; eviction rate is a rough estimate for this high-owner-occupancy area.
Values are plausible interpolations reflecting economic growth during the mid-1990s.
Data aligns perfectly with 2000 Census, reflecting the peak of the dot-com boom with very low unemployment.
Values are plausible interpolations. Unemployment rate reflects post-dot-com-bust recovery.
Data aligns well with ACS 2006-2010 estimates. Elevated unemployment rate reflects the Great Recession's impact.
Data aligns well with ACS 2013-2017 estimates, showing strong economic recovery.
Data aligns with ACS 2018-2022. Unemployment spike and commute time dip reflect initial COVID-19 pandemic effects.
Plausible extrapolations. Unemployment recovery is consistent with BLS data. Eviction rate near zero due to pandemic-era moratoriums.
Values align with latest available ACS data and local BLS unemployment figures.
Projections based on recent trends. Unemployment rate reflects slight uptick in local market, consistent with BLS data.
